IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: Southern Indian Health Council is a Native American organization committed to protecting and improving the physical, mental, and spiritual health of our American Indian Community. We provide a comprehensive range of wellness, health care, dental and other health services.
IRS990.MissionDesc: Southern Indian Health Council is a Native American organization committed to protecting and improving the physical, mental, and spiritual health of our American Indian Community. We provide a comprehensive range of wellness, health care, dental and other health services.
IRS990.Desc: FY2023-2024:We ushered in the new year with the addition of a Chief Medical Officer of Quality Care that will facilitate and build upon SIHCs new vision of zero harm and patient safety. In March, SIHC launched the private screening of our new documentary Everlasting Steps: The Path to Healing" in a big screen theater! This documentary was created to raise awareness for opioid and substance use in Native communities and was funded by the IHS COIPP grant, Award #H1H5IHS0025. The Boys & Girls Club AT&T Connected Learning Center. This provides enhances education for our youth. SIHC gave to the youth over 400 backpacks to the youth. We provided to the community the 26th Annual Native American Health and Wellness Conference, bringing together leaders and advocates to empower our community. During the Halloween season, we held our annual Trunk or Treat event which was a huge success, offering a safe and fun way for families to have some fun and gather resources. Our action-packed annual Winter Wonderland event had our largest turnout yet while bringing some holiday cheer to our community. We also had a joyous Elders Christmas Party and Native American Veterans Breakfast which were both heartwarming celebrations, honoring the wisdom and contributions of our elders and veterans. This year brought back optometry services that includes eye exams, glasses prescriptions, early prevention for overall eye health. The mobile unit called, ROAM, enhanced services with Starlink capabilities to support rural access to the internet.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Council, pursuant to a determination letter from the Internal Revenue Service, is exempt from Federal and State income taxes under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue code and Section 23701(d) of the California Revenue and Taxation Code. Therefore, no provision for income taxes has been made.The accounting standard on accounting for uncertainty in income taxes addresses the determination of whether tax benefits claimed or expected to be claimed on a tax return should be recorded in the financial statements. Under that guidance, the Organization may recognize the tax benefit from an uncertain tax position only if it is more likely than not that the tax position will be sustained on examination by taxing authorities based on the technical merits of the position. Examples of tax positions include the tax-exempt status of the Organization and various positions related to the potential sources of unrelated business taxable income (UBIT). The tax benefits recognized in the financial statements from such a position are measured based on the largest benefit that has a greater than 50% likelihood of being realized upon ultimate settlement. There were no unrecognized tax benefits identified or recorded as liabilities at September 30, 2024.

OtherNonCashContriTableGrp.Desc: Reduced cost of meal for MOW p

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, Line 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization is comprised of seven tribes, each with a delegate and an alternative delegate.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The 990 is prepared by the independent public accountant and is reviewed by the board of directors, CEO, COO, and CFO prior to filing.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The conflict of interest policy is reviewed annually with all board members to identify potential conflicts of interests during the board meetings. If there is a potential for conflict on any particular voting board member, the individual board member(s) is excluded from the vote.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 15a
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Compensation is determine by salary survey completed by SIHC director of human resources using comparable data and approved by the board of directors. The board also approved a cost of living increase for all eligible employees.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 15b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Most recent compensation determined by salary survey completed bt SIHC director of human resources using comparable data in August 2014 and approved by the board of directors.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, Line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization makes its governing documents, conflict of interest policy, and financial statements available to the public upon request.
